deep_dive.title: DEFENGRAPH: Knowledge Graph-Enhanced LLMs for Blue Team Cyber Defense
deep_dive.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise for supporting decision-making in cybersecurity, but their reliability in high-stakes, time-evolving environments remains limited due to hallucinations, poor temporal reasoning, and shallow grounding in system context. We introduce DEFENGRAPH, an LLM-driven assistant designed to support human defenders during cybersecurity incidents. DEFENGRAPH improves contextual reasoning by integrating a dual-layer Static-Dynamic Knowledge Graph (KG) with graph-based path retrieval, LLM-driven contextual filtering, and reasoning-based re-ranking. The framework grounds LLM outputs in both long-term domain knowledge and evolving event context, enabling faithful and temporally aware decision support. We evaluate DEFENGRAPH in a cyber defense setting using knowledge graphs constructed from heterogeneous security artifacts, including SIEM alerts, system topology, attacker behaviors, and prior defensive actions. The evaluation uses data collected during live Red vs. Blue team cyber range exercises simulating attacks on critical infrastructure, which generate realistic and noisy datasets reflecting real-world defender workflows and system dynamics. Evaluations across four prevalent LLMs show that DEFENGRAPH sets a new state-of-the-art: on GPT-4o it boosts reasoning-recall from 61.45\% to 73.49\% and ticket-action recall from 52.17% to 72.46% (precision 24.49\% to 29.24\%), with similar gains on LLaMA-3 (46.99\% to 61.45\%), DeepSeek-R1 (45.78\% to 56.63\%) and QWen-3 (51.81\% to 59.04\%), while surfacing up to 50 correct defense actions versus 36 for the next best baseline and holding fault rates steady.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Integrates knowledge graph with LLM for enhanced cyber defense reasoning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 整合知识图谱与大语言模型以增强网络防御推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 整合知识图谱与大语言模型以增强网络防御推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 整合知识图谱与大语言模型以增强网络防御推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper presents DEFENGRAPH, an LLM-driven assistant that improves cybersecurity decision-making by integrating a dual-layer static-dynamic knowledge graph with graph-based retrieval and reasoning, grounding LLM outputs in domain knowledge and event context.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型在网络安全决策中幻觉和上下文不足的问题，本文提出DEFENGRAPH，通过集成双层静动态知识图谱、图路径检索和推理重排序，增强LLM的上下文推理能力。实验表明该方法能提供忠实且时序感知的决策支持，为知识图谱增强LLM的安全应用提供了框架。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对大语言模型在网络安全决策中幻觉和上下文不足的问题，本文提出DEFENGRAPH，通过集成双层静动态知识图谱、图路径检索和推理重排序，增强LLM的上下文推理能力。实验表明该方法能提供忠实且时序感知的决策支持，为知识图谱增强LLM的安全应用提供了框架。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: graph reasoning capabilities of large language models

quick_skim.title: All Relations Lead to Rome: Automated Knowledge Graph Creation and Question Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models have substantially improved information retrieval and question answering; however, existing datasets generally support either vector-based retrieval over unstructured text or reasoning over knowledge graphs, without providing a unified representation that combines both paradigms. Moreover, current benchmarks rarely provide ground-truth entities, relations, and fact-grounded question-answer pairs aligned with the underlying corpus. To address this gap, we introduce All Relations Lead to Rome (ARLtR), a unified framework for automated knowledge graph construction and fact-grounded question-answer generation. ARLtR jointly constructs a knowledge graph, embeddings, and question-answer pairs that are explicitly grounded in extracted entities, relations, and supporting textual evidence. We further instantiate the framework as a historical dataset centered on the Roman Empire, comprising over 19,000 entities, 16,000 chunks, and 8,400 question-answer pairs (https://huggingface.co/datasets/FaynePro/all-relations-lead-to-rome). By tightly coupling symbolic graph representations with dense retrieval representations, ARLtR facilitates the evaluation and development of hybrid retrieval systems and semantic steering approaches within a single coherent resource.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses LLMs to construct knowledge graphs for unified reasoning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用LLM构建知识图谱用于统一推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用LLM构建知识图谱用于统一推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用LLM构建知识图谱用于统一推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ARLtR framework uses LLMs to jointly construct knowledge graphs, embeddings, and fact-grounded QA pairs, bridging text-based retrieval and graph-based reasoning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有数据集无法同时支持文本向量检索与知识图谱推理的问题，ARLtR框架利用大型语言模型联合构建知识图谱、嵌入表示和事实驱动的问答对，实现了两种范式的统一。该框架自动生成带有实体、关系标注的语料库，为图增强语言模型提供高质量训练数据。实验表明，其生成的问答对能有效桥接文本与图结构信息，促进后续图推理任务。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有数据集无法同时支持文本向量检索与知识图谱推理的问题，ARLtR框架利用大型语言模型联合构建知识图谱、嵌入表示和事实驱动的问答对，实现了两种范式的统一。该框架自动生成带有实体、关系标注的语料库，为图增强语言模型提供高质量训练数据。实验表明，其生成的问答对能有效桥接文本与图结构信息，促进后续图推理任务。
